source.title: Standard Safety Tables and Figures: Integrated Guide
source.publisher: FDA CDER, Biomedical Informatics and Regulatory Review Science (BIRRS)
source.companion_code: NONE — the guide names no reference implementation; every display carries the placeholder footnote 'Source: [include Applicant source, datasets and/or software tools used]'

tiers.core: Section 2 — generated automatically in every ST&F package
tiers.expanded: Section 3 — deeper/alternate views, also generated automatically
tiers.optional: Section 4 — generated only on review-team request
tiers.appendix: Section 5 — reference criteria tables, not analysis outputs

counts.unnumbered_analog_displays: ~12 (Figure 6 x4 panels, Table 20 x4 panels, Table 35 x4 panels)

figures.title: Kaplan-Meier Plot: Time to Permanent Discontinuation of Treatment
figures.notes: at-risk + cumulative-events strip tables, log-rank, pointwise 95% CI bars

figures.title: Subjects With Adverse Events by System Organ Class
figures.notes: left panel % by arm (dot), right panel risk difference w/ 95% CI (forest); sorted by risk difference; ~24 SOCs

figures.title: Subjects With Adverse Events >=X% in Any Treatment Arm by OCMQ (Narrow)
figures.notes: same dual-panel as F2 at OCMQ level; ~55 terms

figures.title: Kaplan-Meier Plot: Time to Onset of [AE of Interest]
figures.notes: cumulative incidence; not informative if N<8 per arm; one per AE of interest

figures.title: Proportion of Subjects Remaining in Trial at Each Visit by Availability of [Lab Value] Result
figures.notes: solid bar = has result, open bar = missing, height = % remaining; x = discrete visit

figures.title: Mean General Chemistry Data Change From Baseline Over Time by Treatment Arm
figures.notes: mean change + 95% CI error bars by visit/arm; extreme values excluded; <10%-data visits dropped

figures.title: Hepatocellular Drug-Induced Liver Injury Screening Plot
figures.notes: eDISH: x = max ALT/AST xULN, y = max TB xULN; quadrants Potential Hy's Law / Cholestasis / Temple's Corollary; 30-day pairing window

figures.title: Cholestatic Drug-Induced Liver Injury Screening Plot
figures.notes: x = max ALP xULN, y = max TB xULN; 2xULN/2xULN cutoffs

figures.title: Mean Vital Sign Data Change From Baseline Over Time by Treatment Arm
figures.notes: same design as F6; SBP, DBP, HR, RR, temperature

figures.title: Box Plot: Median and Interquartile Range of Vital Sign Data Over Time by Treatment Arm
figures.notes: box = IQR, whiskers = 1.5xIQR, outlier points; mean + n tables below

figures.title: Kaplan-Meier Plot: Cumulative Incidence of [AE] Over Time With Pointwise 95% Confidence Interval (OLE)
figures.notes: drug curve continues through OLE; control truncates at end of randomized period

figures.title: Rate of [AE] Over Time (OLE)
figures.notes: incidence rate per 100 PY with 95% CI over discrete intervals (0-12, 12-24, ... >48 wk)

figures.title: Kaplan-Meier Plot: Time to Last Follow-Up
Kaplan-Meier Plot: Time to Onset of [AE]
figures.notes: generalization of F4 to any AE/SAE on request

figures.title: Baseline vs. Maximum/Minimum Postbaseline [Lab Value] by Treatment Arm
figures.notes: scatter + per-arm linear regression + no-change diagonal; separate figure for min

figures.title: Median and Interquartile Range for General Chemistry Data Over Time by Treatment Arm
Median and Interquartile Range for Kidney Function Data Over Time by Treatment Arm
Median and Interquartile Range for Liver Biochemistry Data Over Time by Treatment Arm
Median and Interquartile Range for Lipid Data Over Time by Treatment Arm

Proportion of Subjects Remaining in Trial at Each Visit by Availability of [Vital Sign] Result
Baseline vs. Maximum/Minimum Postbaseline [Vital Sign] by Treatment Arm

cross_cutting_rules: Title suffix convention: every display ends '..., Safety Population, Pooled Analysis (or Trial X)'
Every display carries: Source footnote, TEAE-definition + MedDRA-version note, Duration note, alphabetized Abbreviations line
Risk Difference % (95% CI) is the standard treatment-effect statistic; NOT multiplicity-adjusted; descriptive/signal-detection only
Default ordering is by decreasing risk difference, not alphabetical
Populations: ITT, mITT, Safety (>=1 dose), Per-protocol; Safety is default for AE/lab/VS displays
OCMQ (OND Custom Medical Query, formerly FMQ): Narrow = default, Broad = on request; broad subsumes narrow; never sum narrow+broad (double counting)
Each OCMQ maps to exactly one organ system by clinical judgment even when it spans MedDRA SOCs
Extreme-value exclusion: values beyond Tables 58/59/60 thresholds removed from mean-change displays as likely lab error
Sparse-data rule: visits with data from <10% of subjects in all arms excluded from change-over-time displays
Unscheduled visits mapped to nearest protocol-specified visit; approach stated in footnote
Sex-specific displays restrict the denominator (Ns) to that sex
KM convention: at-risk + cumulative-event strip tables beneath every KM plot, log-rank, pointwise 95% CI bars, earliest qualifying event = event time
Box plot convention: box = IQR, line = median, whiskers = 1.5xIQR, points beyond = outliers; mean + n tables beneath
Shift plot convention: gray dotted diagonal = no change, solid line = drug-arm regression, gray dashed = control-arm regression
DILI plot convention: quadrant cutoffs TB 2xULN with ALT/AST 3xULN (hepatocellular) or ALP 2xULN (cholestatic), 30-day default pairing window
Established grading systems (DAIDS for HIV, CTCAE for oncology) may substitute for Table 56/57 criteria
Separate ST&F packages per registration trial or per substantially different trial design
